Mar 22
Daily Intelligence Brief

US–Iran Conflict

Saturday, March 22, 2026
Automated via Claude Code
Escalation
4 / 5
01

Breaking Developments

Mar 21–22, 2026
Mar 22 AM UTC Trump threatens to hit and obliterate Iranian power plants if Tehran does not reopen the Strait of Hormuz within 48 hours — a major escalation in rhetoric
Mar 21 PM UTC Iran announces it will allow Japanese vessels to transit the Strait of Hormuz after diplomatic consultations, expanding the selective blockade exemption list
Mar 21 PM UTC Trump says administration considering winding down military efforts — yet deploys 2,500 additional Marines to the region and requests more Congressional funding
Mar 21 AM UTC Two waves of Iranian drones strike Kuwait's Mina al-Ahmadi refinery — one of the Middle East's largest (730,000 bpd capacity), sparking major fire
Mar 21 AM UTC Saudi Arabia intercepts 20 Iranian drones over eastern provinces near major oil installations; Bahrain reports destroying 143 missiles, 242 drones since Feb 28
Mar 20 PM UTC US temporarily lifts sanctions on Iranian oil already loaded on ships — adds ~140M barrels to global supply; valid until April 19, 2026
Mar 20 AM UTC Israel reports continued Iranian missile fire; Netanyahu threatens more attacks after a strike on the city of Arad
02

Analysis & Strategic Highlights

Military
Iran's "Escalate, Don't Calibrate" Doctrine
CSIS analysis reveals Iran has pursued unbridled escalation rather than calibrated retaliation, launching attacks across 12+ countries and rendering the Middle East a "no-go zone." This horizontal escalation spanning hundreds of miles is unprecedented in modern conflict.
Diplomatic
UN Security Council Fractures Over Sanctions
Russia and China challenged the legality of snapback sanctions at a March 12 session. The Council voted 11-2 to proceed with Iran nuclear program debates, but the divide undermines unified international pressure on Tehran.
Humanitarian
1,444 Killed — Including 204 Children
Amnesty International condemned the strike on a Minab elementary school that killed 170+ people, mostly schoolgirls. The Iranian Red Crescent reports over 6,668 civilian units targeted by US-Israeli strikes. At least 56 museums and historic sites damaged.
International
Global Oil Crisis: Brent Surges Past $110
Oil prices surged from $73 to $120+ per barrel since Feb 28 — a 40%+ spike. The US war effort costs ~$900M/day. Japan has released strategic reserves. Strait of Hormuz traffic down from 100+ ships/day to just 21 tankers total since war began.
Military
Trump's 48-Hour Power Plant Ultimatum
Trump's threat to "hit and obliterate" Iran's power plants unless the Strait of Hormuz reopens within 48 hours marks a new escalation threshold — targeting civilian energy infrastructure could trigger further Iranian retaliation.
Diplomatic
IAEA Locked Out of Iran's Nuclear Sites
The IAEA confirmed damage to entrance buildings at Natanz enrichment facility. Iran holds 440.9 kg of 60%-enriched uranium at Isfahan. The agency has no access to any of Iran's four declared enrichment facilities — a verification blackout.
03

OSINT & Data Tools

5 resources
Shor73/war-israel-usa-vs-iran-monitor
Advanced
Real-time OSINT war room dashboard — missile stats, casualty tracker, theater map, Tzeva Adom alerts, and economic impact visualization.
Comprehensive situational awareness dashboard purpose-built for the 2026 Iran conflict.
danielrosehill/Iran-Israel-War-2026-OSINT-Data
Easy
Structured dataset tracking Iranian aerial warfare attack waves using AI-assisted research and publicly available OSINT sources.
Clean, queryable data on every documented drone and missile attack wave since Feb 28.
IranWarLive.com
Easy
Automated OSINT engine on serverless infrastructure — live map tracking missile strikes, IDF operations, airspace closures, and real-time casualty data.
Low-friction, browser-based situational awareness with no setup required.
GlobalConflictAwareness.com
Easy
Real-time OSINT dashboard pulling from 89 sources (26 Telegram + 63 RSS) in 17 languages. Interactive map with 400+ geolocated strike events.
Widest source coverage of any single tracker — essential for cross-referencing claims.
IranMonitor.org
Easy
Near real-time OSINT collection aggregating social media, news agencies, and open broadcast sources for crisis timeline understanding.
Best for maintaining chronological timeline understanding as events develop rapidly.
04

🐦 X / Twitter
OSINT Tracking Active
@GeoConfirmed, @Bellingcat, and @IntelDoge actively geolocating strikes across Iran and the Gulf. Multiple new verified strike locations added daily.
Nuclear Verification Watch
@ArmsControlWonk tracking IAEA damage assessments at Natanz and Isfahan. Key thread on what the IAEA verification blackout means for nonproliferation.
Trump's Hormuz Ultimatum Dominates
Trump's threat to "obliterate" Iranian power plants and calling NATO allies "cowards" for not helping open Hormuz has become the dominant news driver. Analysts debate whether this is posturing or genuine escalation intent.
💬 Reddit r/geopolitics · r/CredibleDefense
Iran's Selective Hormuz Blockade — Strategic Masterstroke or Overreach?
Active discussion on whether allowing China, India, Japan passage while blocking US-allied shipping is fracturing the Western coalition response. Read →
Day 22 Situation Thread
r/CredibleDefense daily thread analyzing military developments — focus on Gulf air defense performance and Iranian drone tactics evolving to overwhelm point defenses. Read →
05

Context Briefing

Key Context

The US-Israeli strikes on Iran began February 28, 2026, killing Supreme Leader Khamenei and dozens of senior officials in what was framed as a campaign to end Iran's nuclear and ballistic missile programs and induce regime change. Iran retaliated with missiles and drones across 12+ countries — including strikes on Israel, US bases, and Gulf state oil infrastructure — effectively closing the Strait of Hormuz, which handles 20% of global seaborne oil trade.


Three weeks in, the conflict has no clear endgame. Trump sends contradictory signals — talking about "winding down" while deploying Marines and threatening power plants. Iran's selective Hormuz blockade — allowing Chinese, Indian, Pakistani, and now Japanese vessels while blocking US-allied shipping — is fracturing the international response and weaponizing energy dependence.


What to watch: (1) Trump's 48-hour ultimatum on power plants — if executed, it would target civilian infrastructure and likely trigger further escalation. (2) Whether Hormuz opens further as Iran negotiates country-by-country passage rights. (3) Iran's nuclear posture given the IAEA verification blackout and 440+ kg of 60%-enriched uranium. (4) Continued proxy drone attacks on Gulf oil infrastructure testing regional air defenses.

US–Iran Conflict Briefing · Automated via Claude Code
Day 22 — Fourth week of hostilities. No ceasefire in sight. Hormuz partially reopening.
